domainDetection.reasoning: The documents consistently discuss customer health, product adoption, satisfaction metrics (NPS, CSAT), onboarding issues, renewal concerns, potential expansion opportunities, SLA terms, and the involvement of executive sponsors in customer relationships. These topics are central to the Customer Success domain, even when financial stakeholders are mentioned in the context of renewal risks.

executionPlanReasoning: The documents highlight critical adoption issues impacting customer health and explicitly state concerns about contract renewal.

CustomerHealthAgent.sentimentSummary: The customer expresses strong dissatisfaction and frustration regarding current adoption and onboarding, leading to significant renewal concerns and requests for executive escalation.
evidence: Several teams are frustrated with onboarding.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. The EBR should include the customer's executive sponsor and your internal VP of Customer Success. Agenda: review ROI achieved, address top concerns, present renewal roadmap. Prepare a slide deck with usage metrics, outcomes delivered, and a 12-month value roadmap.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Expansion and Upsell Opportunity Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: Identify expansion opportunities when: (1) customer adoption is above 70%, (2) they have expressed interest in additional modules, (3) their team has grown. Steps: (1) Prepare a usage report showing value delivered, (2) Present product roadmap with upcoming features, (3) Propose expansion SKUs aligned with their pain points, (4) Involve your AE for commercial negotiation. Target: expand ARR by 20%+ on renewal.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Proactive Renewal Management Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: Initiate renewal conversations 90 days before the renewal date. If auto-renewal is disabled, treat this as a high-priority renewal risk. Steps: (1) Review contract value and discount eligibility, (2) Schedule renewal discovery call with economic buyer, (3) Prepare a Business Value Assessment showing ROI over the contract period, (4) Present renewal proposal with multi-year incentive if applicable.

reasoning.riskSummary: This Enterprise customer is at high risk of non-renewal due to severe dissatisfaction with low adoption (35%) and onboarding frustrations, explicitly stating they may not renew unless adoption improves. The $300,000 contract does not auto-renew and requires immediate executive intervention.
reasoning.opportunitySummary: A conditional expansion opportunity exists if current adoption and value realization issues are successfully addressed.
reasoning.businessContext: A critical Enterprise account is expressing strong dissatisfaction and frustration regarding product adoption and onboarding, leading to significant renewal concerns and requests for executive escalation.
reasoning.priorityFocus: Immediately initiate an Executive Business Review (EBR) within 7 days, involving the customer's executive sponsor and an internal VP of Customer Success, to address high churn risk and dissatisfaction.

recommendations.impact: Enable Executive Business Review scheduling and prepare for critical renewal discussions.

recommendations.impact: Address high churn risk, mitigate severe dissatisfaction, and enable executive-level problem resolution.
